coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Build Phase, 14 weeks out — Monday is your aerobic reset after the weekend's long effort. Keep it easy: 45–60 min in Zone 2, conversational pace, ideally on rolling terrain. If your legs feel flat from travel, drop to 40 min and don't chase pace. The vert work comes later this week; today is about flushing fatigue and protecting tomorrow's quality session.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Train the gut now so it's race-ready for the Julian Alps. On today's easy run, practice taking 60g carbs/hour even though you don't strictly need it — a gel or 500ml of mix at 30 and 60 min. Post-run within 30 min: ~25–30g protein plus carbs (Greek yogurt with honey and berries, or eggs and toast). Building gut tolerance to 60–90g/hr is as trainable as your legs.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: With time-zone travel stacking on training load, anchor sleep first: aim for 7.5–8 hours and get 10 min of morning daylight wherever you wake up to reset your clock. Tonight, foam-roll quads and calves for 5 min and elevate your legs against a wall for 5 — cheap, hotel-friendly recovery that pays off before this week's hill work.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Fourteen weeks out is where the race is quietly won — not in heroics, but in showing up for the unglamorous Monday miles between time zones. The Julian Alps will demand patience on every long climb. Practice that patience now: settle into today's easy pace and trust that consistency, not intensity, is what carries you up 2,500m of alpine vert in September.
